title: Immune cell isolation from mouse brain and spleen for flow cytometry
author: Amandine EVEN

steps.title: Anesthetize the mouse with an intraperitoneal...
steps.instruction: Anesthetize the mouse with an intraperitoneal injection of pentobarbital at 100 mg/kg .

steps.title: Inject intravenously 3 µg BV711-conjugated anti-CD45...
steps.instruction: Inject intravenously 3 µg BV711-conjugated anti-CD45 diluted in 50 µL of saline.

steps.title: Allow 00:03:00 for intravascular labeling prior...
steps.instruction: Allow 00:03:00 for intravascular labeling prior to terminal collection and cervical dislocation.

steps.title: Add the pieces of tissue in...
steps.instruction: Add the pieces of tissue in a 15mL tube containing 4mL of collagenase D ( 2 mg/mL ) and DNase I ( 28 U/mL ) solution.

steps.title: Stop enzyme activity by adding cold...
steps.instruction: Stop enzyme activity by adding cold PBS containing 2 millimolar (mM) EDTA and 2 % (v/v) FBS.

steps.title: Pass the dissociated tissue through a...
steps.instruction: Pass the dissociated tissue through a 100 µm strainer to remove undigested fragments

steps.title: Centrifuge the homogenate at Room temperature...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the homogenate at Room temperature for 200 x g, Room temperature, 2 minutes and then for 300 x g, Room temperature, 3 minutes discarding the supernatant carefully.

steps.title: Plate cells
steps.instruction: Plate cells: Perform staining at 4 °C in canonical round-bottom 96-well plates protected from light.

steps.title: Block nonspecific binding using Fc block solution:
steps.instruction: Block nonspecific binding using Fc block solution:

steps.title: Wash using PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer
steps.instruction: Wash using PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer.

steps.title: Fix cells in 4% PFA (100µL...
steps.instruction: Fix cells in 4% PFA (100µL in each well) for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Set controls
steps.instruction: Set controls: Prepare FMO controls and compensation controls using OneComp eBeads incubated with fluorescent antibodies.

steps.title: Remove myelin:
steps.instruction: Remove myelin:

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 10 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 10 mL of 37 % (v/v) Percoll. This step have to be done with solution kept at Room temperature .

steps.title: Aspirate the myelin layer and wash...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the myelin layer and wash the cell pellet with PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 500 x g, Room...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 500 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with no brake.

steps.title: Count recovered cells using a hemocytometer...
steps.instruction: Count recovered cells using a hemocytometer and proceed to antibody staining.

steps.title: Mash the tissue through a 100...
steps.instruction: Mash the tissue through a 100 µm strainer using the thumb-rest of a syringe plunger.

steps.title: Rapidly remove the brain and mince tissue with a scalpel
steps.instruction: Rapidly remove the brain and mince tissue with a scalpel.

steps.title: Collect spleen from the same mouse...
steps.instruction: Collect spleen from the same mouse and keep it in ice-cold PBS 1X.

steps.title: Rinse the filter several times with...
steps.instruction: Rinse the filter several times with PBS-EDTA-FBS dissociation buffer to maximize recovery.

steps.title: Centrifuge homogenates at 1500 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge homogenates at 1500 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes .

steps.title: Resuspend splenocytes in 3 mL RBC...
steps.instruction: Resuspend splenocytes in 3 mL RBC lysis buffer and incubate for 00:03:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Wash with PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Wash with PBS-EDTA-FBS buffer and centrifuge again for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Resuspend in 1 mL dissociation buffer,...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1 mL dissociation buffer, count cells, and reserve 2 million cells per spleen for antibody staining.

steps.title: Every 00:15:00 , triturate with a...
steps.instruction: Every 00:15:00 , triturate with a glass pipette, keep cells in a separate tube and add fresh dissociation solution. Repeat 3 times and reduce the size of the glass pipette opening with each new repetition.

steps.title: Put the tube for agitation in...
steps.instruction: Put the tube for agitation in a water bath at 37 °C for a total time of 00:45:00 .

steps.title: Distribute brain and spleen cells in...
steps.instruction: Distribute brain and spleen cells in different plates according to the experiment layout.

steps.title: Centrifuge the plate at 2500 rpm,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the plate at 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 40 seconds and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in fixable viability...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in fixable viability dye eFluor450 (diluted at 1:1000 in 1X PBS).

steps.title: Incubate for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Wash, centrifuge the plate at 2500...
steps.instruction: Wash, centrifuge the plate at 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 40 seconds and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend cells in 50 µL of...
steps.instruction: Resuspend cells in 50 µL of anti-CD16/32 Fc-blocking solution at 0.5 µg per million cells for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Add 50 µL of the antibody...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL of the antibody cocktail and incubate for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Centrifuge 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 40 seconds , discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Centrifuge 2500 rpm, 4 °C, 40 seconds , discard supernatant.

steps.title: Select a flow cytometer with lasers...
steps.instruction: Select a flow cytometer with lasers and detectors compatible with the chosen fluorophores. Here, data were acquired using a BD LSRFortessa X-20.

steps.title: Acquire samples using appropriate instrument settings (e
steps.instruction: Acquire samples using appropriate instrument settings (e.g., voltages and compensation).
